prompt: A clean isometric 3D render that turns a single abstract statistic into a physical object you can feel the weight of — soft studio lighting, matte clay materials, gentle ambient occlusion shadows, a neutral concrete-grey backdrop. The point is scale shock: take a number nobody can intuit and rebuild it as one tangible mass beside a familiar object for comparison (all the gold ever mined as a single cube next to a school bus). One precise, oversized hand-set numeral anchors the composition, with a short caption in clean grotesque type stating the metric and its source year. Subtle measurement bars and a tiny human silhouette provide scale. No clutter — generous negative space, one hero object, one comparison. The spirit: a science-museum exhibit model that reframes a statistic as architecture, so a figure you'd scroll past becomes something your body understands.
